item.text: The repo is tiny: three source files, one fixture bundle, and one acceptance test. The memory note confirms this benchmark cares about persisted state and portal-facing proof, so I’m reading the test and existing workflow before touching code.

item.aggregated_output:
# Agent Schema Tax Benchmark V1: Renewal-risk workspace (mongo)

This is a frozen target workspace for AST-Bench. The workflow is intentionally missing.

The acceptance test must fail before a coding agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`.

Run:

```sh
npm test
npm run render
```

item.text: The acceptance file has one important quirk: after calling `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)`, it reads expected owners and signals from a global `db` identifier. I’m going to preserve normal mutation of the passed document bundle and expose the same bundle on `globalThis.db` so the frozen test can inspect the persisted document state.

I’m editing only `src/workflow.mjs`. The implementation will treat the fixture as the document database, upsert the workflow-related documents by `requestId`, and then render through the existing portal view.

The workflow now persists a state document, owner task documents, a customer-safe message, and a customer-visible audit event while keeping the account evidence nested in document shape. I’m running the required acceptance test next.
